result.reason: Workflow orchestration with data transformation logic (grouping, stats computation, fallback handling) that could silently break on edge cases

suggestions.description: Test Phase 3 grouping logic: verify that analyzed results are correctly grouped by priority ('critical','high','medium','low','skip') and that empty groups default to empty arrays
Test summary stats computation: verify needs_unit/needs_integration/hasExisting counts are correct when given a mix of analysis results with various flags
Test fallback handling when results[i] is null/undefined/non-object — should produce a safe default entry with priority 'medium' and empty suggestions
Test total_test_suggestions accumulator handles entries with missing/undefined suggestions arrays (r.suggestions?.length ?? 0 path)

Test full workflow execution with a mock agent that returns structured analysis for a small set of files — verify end-to-end output shape matches expected {summary, critical, high, medium, low, skipped}
Test discovery phase returns early with error object when agent returns an empty array or non-array value for file discovery
Test default args resolution: dir defaults to '.', extensions to 'ts,js,tsx,jsx,py,go,rs', exclude to expected directories when args is undefined/partial
Test pipeline stage correctly maps file list through agent calls and handles partial failures (some files return valid results, others return null)

risk_factors: Grouping logic silently drops files if priority value doesn't match expected strings (e.g. typo or unexpected value from agent)
Summary counts could be wrong if filter predicates don't handle undefined/falsy values from agent responses
Fallback for failed analysis (result is null/non-object) could mask issues — wrong fallback shape would break downstream consumers
Pipeline concurrency issues if agent calls fail partially — results array indices may not align with files array
Non-array discovery response (e.g. agent returns object or string) would cause files.length === 0 early return, losing context about what went wrong

result.reason: Main extension entry point with complex branching tool execute logic (list/status/cancel/start actions) and error handling paths; only formatRun is currently tested.
risk_factors: Cancel action incorrectly marks wrong steps as cancelled (e.g. already-completed steps)
Invalid JSON args not caught properly leading to runtime errors
Workflow not found path fails to suggest available workflows
Run status saved incorrectly on workflow execution failure
Command handler argument parsing breaks on edge cases (spaces in workflow names, empty args)
Session start handler crash if listWorkflows throws (unhandled rejection)
Race condition in step tracking - saveRun called with stale data during concurrent step updates

suggestions.description: Test 'cancel' action only marks pending/running steps as cancelled, leaving completed/failed steps unchanged
Test 'cancel' action returns error when run_id is not provided
Test 'status' action returns formatted run output for a valid run_id
Test 'status' action returns 'not found' when run_id doesn't exist
Test 'start' action returns helpful error with available workflows list when workflow name not found
Test 'start' action returns JSON parse error when args parameter contains invalid JSON
Test 'start' action handles workflow module with no default export gracefully (sets status to completed)
Test 'start' action executes workflow default function, tracks steps via runtime callbacks, and saves final completed state with result
Test 'start' action catches workflow execution error, sets run status to 'failed', and returns error message
Test 'list' action returns template and available workflows with their sources and directories
Test 'list' action shows recent runs (up to 10) with truncated runId, workflow name, status, and date
Test command handler parses 'workflowName arg1 arg2' correctly and sends appropriate user message
Test command handler with no args or 'list' calls ui.select and sends message for selected workflow
Test session_start handler sets UI status badge showing correct workflow count

result.reason: Core workflow discovery/loading logic with filesystem traversal, path resolution, and dynamic imports — bugs here silently break workflow resolution
risk_factors: Workflow discovery could miss directories if path traversal logic has off-by-one errors
Project workflows might not correctly shadow global workflows (dedup via seen set)
loadWorkflow returns null for invalid modules but also for missing meta.name — ambiguous failure
findGitRoot infinite loop risk if dirname behaves unexpectedly on edge-case paths
Extension filtering is case-insensitive (.toLowerCase) but only for specific extensions — easy to break
discoverProjectDirs stops at git root but falls back to / if no git root — could scan entire filesystem tree

suggestions.description: Test getProjectWorkflowDir returns correct .pi/workflows path for given cwd
Test discoverProjectDirs walks up from cwd to git root, producing all PROJECT_DIR_NAMES at each level
Test discoverProjectDirs stops at git root and doesn't traverse beyond it
Test discoverProjectDirs falls back to / boundary when no .git directory exists
Test listWorkflows deduplicates by name, preferring project-scoped (earlier) over global workflows
Test listWorkflows correctly labels entries as 'project' vs 'global' source
Test listWorkflows returns results sorted alphabetically by name
Test listDir filters only .js/.ts/.mjs/.mts files from a real temp directory with mixed file types
Test listDir returns empty array for non-existent directory without throwing
Test loadWorkflow resolves the first matching file across multiple directories and extensions
Test loadWorkflow returns null when module exists but meta.name is missing
Test loadWorkflow returns null when no matching workflow file is found in any directory
Test findGitRoot correctly identifies .git directory walking up from a nested subdirectory
Test full discovery flow: create temp dir structure with .git, project workflows, and global dir, verify correct precedence ordering

result.reason: Core workflow runtime with good existing coverage but the `step` function is completely untested and `createEmitResultTool` schema wrapping logic has gaps
risk_factors: step() error handling differs from agent() (re-throws vs catches) - untested behavior could regress
Non-object schema wrapping/unwrapping in createEmitResultTool could silently break structured output for array/string schemas
The emit_result tool capture path (when tool IS actually called) is not tested end-to-end
Pipeline failure logging could mask errors if log behavior changes

suggestions.description: Test step() executes function and reports running→completed status transitions with correct timestamps and result
Test step() re-throws errors (unlike agent which catches them) while still setting step status to 'failed' with error message
Test step() with async function that resolves - verify step.completedAt > step.startedAt
Test createEmitResultTool with non-object schema (e.g. {type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}) wraps in envelope with 'result' property
Test createEmitResultTool execute() captures params correctly and returns terminate:true - verify getResult() returns the captured value
Test createEmitResultTool with non-object schema unwraps params.result instead of returning full params
Test agent() with schema on non-object type includes 'Pass your result in the result parameter' in prompt
Test agent() when multiple message_end events arrive - verify last text block is used as response
Test agent() ignores non-text content blocks (e.g. tool_use blocks) in message content
Test step() passes name and phase correctly to onStep callback

result.reason: Comprehensive tests already exist covering all exported functions, error paths, sorting, and edge cases like corrupt JSON and non-JSON files

suggestions.description: Test path traversal safety: verify that a runId containing '../' or absolute paths doesn't write outside the runs directory
Test concurrent saveRun calls for the same runId to verify last-write-wins behavior without corruption
Test listRuns performance with a large number of run files (e.g., 100+) to ensure Promise.all doesn't cause issues

risk_factors: Path traversal via malicious runId values could write files outside intended directory
Concurrent writes to the same run file could produce corrupt JSON
No validation that loaded JSON actually conforms to WorkflowRun type shape
